segment-text: OVER BLACK. THE SOUND OF AN ENGINE, SPUTTERING DEAD. CARD: GREEN ROOM
INT. CONVERSION VAN - DAWN

LOCAL RADIO quietly rattles through blown speakers. Four YOUNG MEN lie contorted within, sound asleep. A muted sunbeam cuts through their hanging breath and finds PAT (20s, a doe-eyed vandal) in the rear cargo hold. He blinks awake and sits up, grasping for equilibrium. He turns and wipes the fogged porthole window behind him. THROUGH THE PORTHOLE: BATTERED GREEN STALKS tight against the glass. Shit.
EXT. CORN FIELD - SUNRISE
FROM WAY UP HIGH: The weathered conversion van sits in a cornfield, a wake of trampled stalks marking its thirty yard drift from the road. The landscape is vast, coastal.
INT. CONVERSION VAN - SUNRISE
Pat crawls between the bucket seats and puts a hand on SAM (late 20s, scruffy), asleep in the front passenger seat.
Sam. Wake-up. REECE (20s, a natural athlete), sits up from between EQUIPMENT CASES, encased in a SLEEPING BAG.
What's wrong?
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
Sam wipes the windshield clear, sees the WALL OF CORN.
(slapping the driver) What'd you do, Tiger?
The driver, TIGER (20s, a wiry mutt with dyed blue hair) jolts alert and surveys the scene.
Did we crash?
You tell us, asshole.
Guess I fell asleep.
With the engine running. THE FUEL GAUGE NEEDLE SITS BELOW 'E'. Well done.
Sam disconnects his CELL PHONE from a cigarette lighter adapter. It CHIRPS.
Full charge. Did you kill the battery too?
You hear the radio?
Pat reaches over, yanks the keys and kills the LOCAL RADIO.
EXT. CONVERSION VAN - CORNFIELD - DAY
TRACKING SAM, swiping his phone, rounding the open rear doors of the van as Reece and Tiger unload stickered-up CASES. We see them now. ROCK SHIRTS, TATTOOS, SHITTY HAIRCUTSTHEY'RE A PUNK BAND.
There's a skating rink eleven miles
from here. Big parking lot.
Pat hops from the van, dragging out a battered BMX BIKE.
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
Ice skating or roller skating?
Just says they're open. Why?
Pat pulls a DUFFEL BAG and GAS CAN from the van.
Hockey players whoop more ass.
Dunno, dude- I've seen some pretty badass roller skaters...
At 7am? I'll come with.
Pat nods, shrugs the Duffel over his shoulder.
EXT. HIGHWAY - DAY
Pat pedals the BMX down the highway, Sam sits atop a rear mounted luggage rack transfixed by his phone, feet resting on the BACK WHEEL PEGS.
That thing ruins everything. ...huh?
If we didn't know where we were going, we'd be on a quest. But we do... so it's an errand. It's a serene morning, Sam YAWNS.
EXT. SKATING RINK - PARKING LOT - DAY

PUSHING IN on a gravel lot. The unmanned BMX wheels down a DRAINAGE DITCH and falls over. Pat and Sam follow its path, taking cover in the ditch. Sam scans the lot as Pat removes a SIPHON KIT from the duffel: 1/4" CLEAR TUBING, a length of BLACK RUBBER HOSE, and a stained RAG.
Thar she blows...
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
Near the edge of the lot sits a monster SUV.
EXT. SKATING RINK - PARKING LOT - SUV - MOMENTS LATER

Pat and Sam scamper to a crouch beside the SUV. Sam unscrews the gas cap. Pat feeds one end of the clear tubing into the tank, the other into their canister. He shoves the rubber hose into the tank and presses the rag down as a seal. Pat blows two hard breaths into the hose. Gas TRICKLES then POURS into the canister. Shared grins. A metal CLANK. ECHOES from a cavernous interior. Pat springs up and peers through the TINTED SUV WINDOWS, seeing PARENTS corralling PEE-WEE HOCKEY PLAYERS from a rink access door into the lot. Pat crouches, signals to pack it up. Sam pulls the rig.
EXT. HIGHWAY - DAY
Pat pedals up the highway, Sam on the rack, hunched over his phone. An SUV rounds the corner, its RPMs break the quiet.
(looking up) Uh oh.
Pat turns to see the SUV gaining, CURSES.
What are we doing?
Cover the gas. Get ready to run.
Sam hastily covers the gas canister with his jacket as the SUV slows beside them. They look ahead, playing it not-socool. The SUV window rolls down, revealing a HOCKEY MOM.
Not safe, what you boys did...
(half-assed) We didn't do anything, ma'am.
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
I can smell it. (pointing) That's it, there.
Pat drags his feet to a stop. Hockey Mom follows suit. Now visible are two PEE-WEE HOCKEY PLAYERS pressed to the backseat glass.
We're sorry.
It's dangerous. If you boys needed gas, I would've given it to you.
Oh. Thank you.
Her scolding eyes.
...sincerely.
Next time, you ask. Yes ma'am.
Pat nods earnestly.
You broken down? I can give you a ride.
We'll be fine.
Alright. Be safe, now... and you go with Christ.
The SUV cruises off.
Sam glances to Pat, who makes the sign of the cross and resumes pedaling. Sam signs the cross with his right handBUT GRABS IT WITH HIS LEFT, pantomiming an epic hand fight.
Oh no. Oh dear! (death-metal ghoulish) YEEAAARGHHHH!
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
Sam's left hand wins out and thrusts into the air: his pinky and index fingers protruding from a balled fist: THE MANO CORNUTO: THE HORNED HAND OF SATAN! CUE MAJESTIC METAL ANTHEM.
EXT. CORN FIELD - HIGHWAY SHOULDER - DAY
SWEEPING ABOVE CORN STALKS AS SAM, TIGER AND PAT PUSH THE VAN FROM SUNKEN SOIL AND CHASE IT ONTO THE HIGHWAY SHOULDER.
INT. CONVERSION VAN (MOVING) - HIGHWAY - DAY
WIND WHIPS through the van. MUSIC BLARES, wild hair.
EXT. THE OREGON COAST - DUSK (AERIAL) The van cruises along coastal highway. EXT. BEACH TOWN - MAGIC HOUR
ON A CELL PHONE TOUCH SCREEN:
'Corner of 12th & Ocean_'.

A THUMB TAPS THE REST OF THE TEXT MESSAGE: 'I have a mohowk_' 'SEND'. THE THUMB HASTILY RE-TYPES: 'Mohawk_' 'SEND'. TILT UP to reveal the conversion van GROWL up to the corner. TAD (l9), in homemade rags sporting a liberty-spiked Mohawk, meets the passenger window as Sam TURNS THE MUSIC DOWN. Sam?
Awesome. Hey, I work nights- but I'll catch up with you guys for breakfast.
Tad hands over HOUSE KEYS on a chain, ties on an APRON.
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
I'm in 2R, up the stairs, just crash wherever. Park in the side lot- rear doors tight to the wall so no one steals your shit.
(looks to Reece, at wheel) Yeah?
Reece raises his eyebrows and the van lurches forward.
INT. TAD'S STUDIO APARTMENT - NIGHT

A cramped refuge scattered with ART SUPPLIES, ZINES, and ROCK PARAPHERNALIA. Reece steals a workout, chair-dipping in the living area. Sam sorts through PACKETS of Ramen Noodles on the kitchenette counter.
These all have mushrooms.
Tiger inspects a bookshelf collection of VINYL LPs.
This dude's legit.
Pat examines a cluster of framed CAT PHOTOS on a dresser. Why?
(finishing his dips) He gets up early to do his hair?
Tiger defensively brushes his hair back and un-sleeves an LP. He's true.
Sam pulls a SIXER from the fridge, tosses one to Reece, who's unplugging the charging phone.
Who you calling?
That your business?
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
I get the bills.
Reece backs down, cracks his beer.
I'm going to bed.
(cracking a beer) We're going to drink.
Tiger seats the LP on a turntable, sets the needle down, and just as the LP CRACKLES TO LIFE with a signature COUNT OFF FROM LEE VINGLEE VING (ON ALBUM) ...1234 1234!
INT. TAD'S APARTMENT - THE NEXT MORNING

CLOSE-UP: THE NEEDLE BOBS AT THE EDGE OF THE SPINNING LP. SOFT IN THE BACKGROUND, Pat stands up out of frame, zombiewalks to the turntable and powers it down. WE TRACK BEHIND PAT as he grabs half-empty BEER CANS and pours them into the kitchenette sink. WE PUSH INTO HIS P.O.V. THROUGH THE WINDOW: Tad climbs the steps two at a time carrying grocery bags. WE FLOAT BEHIND PAT as he opens the door for Tad.
(biting lip) You the first to fall asleep?
INT. TAD'S APARTMENT - BATHROOM - MORNING

Pat is hunched over the sink. He rises, inspecting his face in the mirror, scrubbing it with a moldy bar of soap. ON PAT'S FACE, crudely penned in SHARPIE: A BIG, STUPID MONOCLE AROUND HIS EYE AND CAT WHISKERS ON HIS CHEEKS.
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14) INT. TAD'S APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - MORNING
A compact DIGITAL RECORDER blinks RED on a coffee table. The Band sits huddled on a couch, plates of scrambled eggs on their laps. Tad sits opposite, cross-legged on the floor.
...working on anything new?
A few songs. Maybe enough for a seven-inch.
Sweet! Will you actually press one?
If we can afford it.
I dig the analogue style. Which brings me to the fact: you guys are hard to find. Why no social media presence?
Pat contains a wince. Sam turns to face Tiger.
Because booking more shows and selling more records would blow.
It's not hard-rock.
Says the one who gets smashed and
plays Darby Crash at the shows I
Tiger looks to his plate, eats some eggs.
No one wants to starve, but if you take it all virtual, you lose... the texture.
What do you mean, texture?
Just- you gotta be there. The music is for effect. It's time and aggression and...
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
Technical wizardry.
...t's shared- live. And then it
goes away. The energy- it can't
Unless you're Iggy Pop.
And good for him. I just don't think I'll be in my 70's still listening to Minor Threat.
Tiger will. Right?
I won't live to be seventy.
Reece and Sam blurt out mocking laughs, Pat drifts.
Okay, so this is a good segue into one of my traditions. For each of you, name your 'desert island' band.
Reece rolls his eyes.
If I were to say Black Sabbath, would I get Ozzy and Dio? TAD No caveats- just name the band.
Sam, Reece, Pat and Tiger size each other up...
Misfits. No, The Damned. Misfits.
Poison Idea.
(under breath) Shit man...
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
Cro-Mags.
Pat, shaking his head, stressing out.
Just say something, dude.
Steely Dan. Candlebox.
He's a Juggalo.
Ah, man. (rubs face) Will this be edited?
I'll chop it up a bit.
Let it go raw.
When will this air? Shouldn't we plug the show?
(thrown) Yeah...
He turns off the recorder.
Tiger smiles.
County commissioner got wind and pulled my permit. You were already en route.
Damn dude. We need a kill fee.
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
We went 90 miles out of our way. I've got a backup lined-up. Lunch, 50% cut on the door, and you guys would headline.
Anyone else still on the bill? They bailed.
Deadpan from the Band as GUITAR FEEDBACK BUILDS...
INT. MEXICAN RESTAURANT - DAY

A waterside mexican cafe. No frills except strung-up CHRISTMAS LIGHTS. THE BAND BLASTS OUT A CHARGED, RAPID-FIRE PUNK SONG. Sam teases FEEDBACK from an AMP with his LES PAUL. Pat on BASS, eyes trained on the floor. Behind a DRUM KIT, Reece strikes with precision. TIGER SCREAMS into a MIC. ON THE ETHNICALLY DIVERSE CROWD: Less than a dozen. Most of them sitting down. Some eating Mexican from PAPER PLATES. TAKE-OUT CUSTOMERS plugging their ears as they're rung-up. A DIEHARD, slamdancing with himself. Two BACK-PACK KIDS nodding heads. A FAN, archiving the performance with an iPHONE. The Band screeches to a halt with heaving chests.
(exasperated, to the Fan) Turn that shit off...
EXT. MEXICAN RESTAURANT - WATERSIDE LOT - MAGIC HOUR

Tiger rolls CASES to Pat. Pat lifts them into the van. Reece shoves them into place. Tad holds two bags of MEXICAN TAKE-OUT, watching Sam count a meager stack of CRUMPLED BILLS. Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
I gave you my cut. The house got theirs, but I diSAM (finishing the count) Split four ways it's six dollars each.
Six eighty-seven. Eighty-eight if you round up. Which I don'tREECE (charging) You dipshit fashion-punk clown motherFUCKER...
Reece SLAMS Tad against a wall, the take-out drops. Christ.
Easy there, Jujitsu.
Reece presses his forearm heavy against Tad's neck, resisting
(to Reece) Let's not go to jail too. (to Tad) I think you just ended this tour.
Fuck yes. Let's call it.
Make a beeline to D.C.? We've got enough for one tank- it'd be siphoning the rest of the way.
(releasing Tad) Not a problem. (snatching the take-out) We've got rice and beans!
We can head up north and catch 80 all the way.
Reece, Tiger and Sam head for the van.
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
Lemme call my cousin. I can get you a solid gig.
Reece mounts the van, SLAMS the door. The rest stick around.
Where? Here?
Scene's dead. You'd have to dip down closer to Portland.
INT./EXT. CONVERSION VAN - TAD'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
Tiger sits shotgun, Sam behind the wheel.
I say we just gank his vinyl. Pat and Reece sit eyeballing each other in the back of the IDLING VAN. Sam's phone CHIMES as Tad jogs up to the window.
It's set. Matinee tomorrow, Doors at one, you're on at three. I texted the addy.
$350, minus your tab. The band plays it down, but this is a windfall.
And just so you know, it's mostly boots and braces down there.
Skins? There's some at every show.
SHARP? DMS? Sabre?
Right wing- or, technically ultra left- but not affiliated. (MORE)
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
But your cousin is cool?
Yeah- don't talk politics, but stick with Daniel. I'd tag along but he and his girl are coming here to crash. Gotta vacuum and shit.
But no one's burning crosses or anything- we just play rock?
I'd play your earlier stuff. Heavier stuff.
(refined accent) These gentlemen like to mosh.
Girls too. It's sorta the only scene in town. Here...
Tad hands Tiger a black and white FLYER.
I usually keep the originals, but since this one never happened...
Tiger tucks the flyer away.
Can I still run that interview...?
Yeah- what station is it on?
FM eighty-five-five- "Breakfast of Champions". Thanks.
Tiger dials in the station, Sam puts the van in gear and Pat smacks Reece's arm as they pull out.
...sorry I almost obliterated you! TAD (O.S.) Not a problemmmmmm...
INT. CONVERSION VAN (MOVING) - ROUTE 26 EAST - NIGHT

PITCH BLACK. The WHIR of the road. A FLASHLIGHT BEAM POPS ON, SCANS ACROSS HAND DRAWN IMAGES: Horned skulls, fleshy tendrils and a liquid font of zombie vomit: '$5 Matinee! Missionary Position, Ain't Rights, NTOF Sat 1pm Seaside Municipal Center'
At least dude can draw.
Tiger hands TAD'S FLYER and the flashlight back to Pat. They're all eating rice and beans with plastic forks. Pat inspects the flyer and offers it to Reece.
Pretty sweet.
Reece looks up from the flyer, the glow from the flashlight up-lighting his face, and gives Pat a soul-penetrating stare.
There's something I never told you. Anyone, for that matter...
What's that?
Holding the stare, Reece RIPS A FART. The Van ERUPTS INTO LAUGHTER as Reece tosses the flyer and Pat pounces.
EXT. TURN OFF - MORNING
The van passes an APPLE ORCHARD and turns down a two-lane road bordered by dense inland foliage.
INT. CONVERSION VAN - MORNING
CU: ON SAM'S PHONE: Mapping Software tracks their position. Nothing else around.
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14) EXT. TWO LANE ROAD - DAY (AERIAL)
FROM ABOVE, TRACKING THE VAN as it's swallowed by woods.
EXT. RESIDENCE - DAY
FROM BEHIND a closed ENTRY GATE, with a SIGN facing the road. The van whips by...
INT. CONVERSION VAN - DAY

THROUGH THE WINDSHIELD: the narrow road opens into a cul-desac of dirt and trampled grass. On the far end lies THE VENUE, a converted public utility building of brick and sandstone with a cheap-siding addition extending from the rear. The lot is cluttered with 4x4s, WEATHERED SEDANS and TRICKEDOUT HATCHBACKS. ROCKERS and SKINHEADS trickle into the venue.
EXT. THE VENUE - LOT - DAY

The van rolls up and the Band dismounts, gulping fresh air as they're greeted by DANIEL (27), bomber jacket, boots and grown-in buzz cut, breaking from a group of TAILGATERS admiring a pale gold '65 PONTIAC RAGTOP at the lot's edge.
You guys Tad's friends?
We- he sent us. Cousin Dan?
(shaking hands) Daniel. You guys look hammered.
GABE (30s), crew cut and a general disdain, approaches cradling a clip board.
One night at Tad's will do it. And
if your girl is crashing tDaniel grabs Sam and presses their foreheads together.
(quiet, through a smile) Do not mention that.
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
No worries whatsoever. Just shut the fuck up about him and me and her.
Daniel releases shaken Sam with a friendly pat.
(arriving) Who's the Drummer? Me.
Just bring your cymbals- you're using the house kit. Okay.
Tiger motions to a church-style SIGN out front: In encased changeable letters: '1PM DOORS: COWCATCHER, KOKYTUS, AREN'T RIGHTS' Before Tiger speaks up, Pat calls him off with a look.
INT. THE VENUE - BACKSTAGE - DAY

HARD ROCK over the PA. MOVING DOWN A DARK NARROW HALLWAY: lugging gear, the Band follows Gabe through a backstage corridor. Gabe rounds a corner and gestures to BIG JUSTIN (26), a tattooed heavyweight who dutifully steps aside for the procession. Gabe stops at a graffiti-covered door.
(over music) STAGE IN HERE. KEEP THE HALLWAY
CLEAR- OWNER DOESN'T FUCK AROUND WITH FIRE CODE. SOUND CHECK IN GOT IT.
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14) INT. GREEN ROOM - DAY MUFFLED ROCK. A fluorescent-lit interior with carpet

remnants, a ratty couch, a bare-bulb Formica makeup counter, and a coffin-sized bathroom with curtains for a door. Twodecades of history told in BAND STICKERS and SHARPIE SCRAWL. Sam yanks ELECTRIC HAIR CLIPPERS from the counter-top outlet and plugs in his cell phone charger. CHIRP. You okay?
Yeah. Are these guys not creeps?
Tiger and Pat strap on GUITARS, scanning the GRAFFITI SCRAWL. Tiger finds a SWASTIKA, looks to Pat.
(stacking cases) Run a tight ship.
But it's a U-boat...
Hey ya'll. I got a dumb idea.
INT. THE VENUE - STAGE - DAY

Sun pours through the high casement windows, side-lighting the gathering crowd. Lots of shaved heads and bomber jackets. STAGEHAND Where'd you say the power supplSAM'S CRUNCHING GUITAR INTERRUPTS. Sound check fiddle-faddle as the PA MUSIC fades out. It's like a mini-transformer, with three females- like an XLR, but notPat plays a familiar BASS RIFF. GOT IT.
STAGEHAND
More GUITAR CRUNCH. Then a LOW, RHYTHMIC TOM DRUM MARCH. Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
(into mic) TEST. ONE-TWO. MEOW. More guitar on mine, please.
His monitor turns up, then a look to Reece. Reece nods back. The DRUM MARCH BUILDS.
(into mic) Thank you.
TUNING guitars, Sam and Pat lean in for an off-mic aside.
WE'RE NOT, ARE WE?
YOUR IDEA. YOU BACK OUT NOW, I TELL THEM YOU'RE JEWISH. (to Tiger) GO!
(into mic) Okay, everybody! We're the Ain't Rights. Or the Aren't Rights. Either one-TWO-THREE-FOUR!!!
THE BAND GRINDS STRAIGHT INTO a classic DEAD KENNEDYS SONG:
(singing into mic) Punk ain't no religious cult Punk means thinkin' for yourself You ain't hardcore 'cuz you spike your hair, When a jock still lives inside your head...
The crowd barely sways, those who know the song give cold stares...
Nazi punks, Nazi punks Nazi punks- Fuck Off!
Nazi punks, Nazi punks
Now some BOOS and middle fingers.
If you've come to fight, get outta here, You ain't no better than the bouncers, We ain't trying to be police When you ape the cops it ain't anarchy..
Carving through the crowd, a towering skinhead, WERM (28) appears leading a pack of four: two GUYS, wearing jackets with white stenciled 'COWCATCHER' logos, and two GIRLS.
Nazi punks,Nazi punks
Nazi punks, Nazi punks

Making his way across the half-stunned pit, Werm glances up at the stage, amused. Behind him, EMILY (20s), with a Chelsea hairdo, crosses paths with Daniel, who discreetly passes her a FOLDED NAPKIN and disappears into the crowd. Her eyes meet Pat's and he is struck. She flips him off. AMBER (20s), lights a cigarette, steering her back on course.
Ten guys jump one, what a man
A bottle SMASHES on stage, Sam and Pat share nervous smiles. Tiger flubs some lyrics, Reece drums too fast to notice. TIGER (CONT'D)

Pat searches for the Emily, but Werm's pack is gone. He finds Daniel, arms crossed at the exit, watching him. Pat joins Sam off-mic for the chorus: TIGER / SAM / PAT NAZI PUNKS, NAZI PUNKS NAZI PUNKS- FUCK OFF! TIGER / SAM / PAT (CONT'D) NAZI PUNKS, NAZI PUNKS NAZI PUNKS- FUCK OFF! The rowdy crowd SHOUTS BACK. Another BOTTLE flies on stage. TIGER
AND THEY GRIND TO A HALT, sweat drenched, tension in the air.
(into mic) Thank you. That was a cover. (off mic to the Band) Whaddya wanna do next?
Reece pounds a BROODING DRUM BEAT. The Band trades looks, shrugging acceptance. Sam and Pat TUNE DOWN their guitars, and the tone goes evil...
Here's a treat...

THEY PLUNGE INTO CORONARY- A FOOT-STOMPING, HARDCORE ASSAULT. The BREAKDOWN hits and the CROWD ERUPTS: LIVE SOUND IS CONSUMED BY A SUBLIME, QUIET DRONE: AND WE GO SLOW-MO... THRASHING BODIES. HARD-LIT SWEAT. SILENT SCREAMS. THE BAND ON STAGE, ELECTRIFIED. TIGER. SAM. PAT. REECE. PERFECT SYNC. THE PIT ON FIRE...
INT. THE VENUE - BACKSTAGE - DAY

PUSHING DOWN THE HALLWAY, THE DRONE FADES... ... INTO EIGHTIES THRASH METAL blasting over the PA. END SLO-MO. Gabe hands Big Justin a WAD OF BILLS by the green room entrance and jogs down an adjacent hallway carrying a GROCERY BAG, its plastic contents rattling within.
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
The Band rounds the corner, sidestepping THEIR OWN STENCILED GEAR, now neatly stacked along the hallway.
(over music) WHAT HAPPENED TO 'FIRE CODE'?
(counting bills)
HEADLINERS.
(handing over bills) YUP. GOTTA CLEAR OUT.
Sam takes the cash and looks to Reece, who thumbs-up an equipment inventory.
TIGHT SHIP. FOLLOW ME.
BIG JUSTIN
Big Justin grabs a case and leads them away. Tiger, Reece, Sam and Pat grab cases, rolling out in unison. Sam stops and pats his pockets.
Shit. My phone... WHAT?
MY PHONE. I'LL CATCH UP.
Sam tries to squeeze by but Pat holds up a hand. I GOT YOU.
Pat whips around and heads back.
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
Big Justin turns to roll his case over a floor seam and spots Pat moving toward the green room. HEY! STOP!
BIG JUSTIN
Hearing only THRASH METAL, Pat casually turns the knob, KNOCKING as he pushes the door open into the room. Big Justin barrels towards him, encumbered by gear... BIG JUSTIN (CONT'D) MOTHER FUCK.
INT. GREEN ROOM - DAY
Pat goes straight for Sam's phone.
(yanking the charger)
Pardon me, ya'llFour PEOPLE and not a word. All eyes on Pat.
Bent awkwardly on the couch is Emily, a BUCK KNIFE DRIVEN DEEP INTO HER SKULL. Very little blood. Holy shit.
PAT (CONT'D)
Werm looks up, flushed but relaxed. Amber stands horror-struck in the corner.
Call the police.
Werm shrugs indifferent.
Big Justin BURSTS into the room.
I TOLD YOU... (big breaths) ...to follow.
Pat dry swallows. Then BOLTS TO THE DOOR WITH THE PHONE.
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
Big Justin grabs a fistful of hoodie, RIPPING it off as Pat scrambles past...
INT. THE VENUE - BACKSTAGE - DAY - CONTINUOUS
Pat leaps across the threshold with a stretched t-shirt, DIALING and flailing, the charger dangling from the phone. GO! GO!
PAT
The Band catches on as Big Justin emerges, eyes bulging. Avoiding their strewn gear, Pat re-directs down the adjacent hall, the Band right behind.
INT. THE VENUE - BACK HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
(into cell) YES, IT IS...
Gabe appears at the far end of the hallway with the DRUMMER.
(into cell)
They sprint to intercept.
PAT (CONT'D)

THERE'S BEEN A STABBING, SHGabe SMACKS the phone from Pat and the DRUMMER follows through with a BRUTE-FORCE SHOVE. Gabe dives for the cell: ON THE PHONE: '911: 00:14...00:15...' Gabe ends the call and pops up.
GOD DAMMIT.
Big Justin brings up the rear, the Band caught mid-hallway. They di- BIG JUSTIN Gabe points to his ear.
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
THEY DIDN'T LOCK THE DOORGABE
NO. DON'T TALK. AND DON'T TOUCH
Gabe speaks into the Drummer's ear. The Drummer nods.
(to Band) I'LL BE RIGHT BACK. DON'T WORRY.
The Drummer backs off and leans against the wall. Big Justin stands opposite, the Guitarist visible over his shoulder in the green room doorway, Amber within, her pleading eyes.
(to Pat) WHAT THE FUCK HAPPENED?
(turning back) GIMME A MINUTE AND SAVE THE TALK.
The Band processing adrenaline.
(to Sam) SOMETHING TERRIBLE.
Pat nods compliance. Gabe disappears with the phone around a corner.
EXT. THE VENUE - AROUND BACK - OFFICE TRAILER - DAY

ON A WIDE: The back door opens and shuts, briefly polluting the quiet air with THRASH METAL. Gabe walks several yards to an OFFICE TRAILER. He disappears for several seconds, re-emerging with an awkward stride...
INT. THE VENUE - BACK HALLWAY - DAY - MOMENTS LATER
ON THE BAND: simmering, sharing looks, METAL still blaring. Breathing regulates... ...until Gabe returns with a RUGER .454 SUPER-REDHAWK REVOLVER by his side.
Pre-Production Draft v.2 (9/07/14)
WAIT. WHAT!? Fuck me.
WHAT IS GOING ON?
DON'T WORRY. (gesturing with gun) JUST GET BACK INSIDE.
Sam's cell RINGS, glowing in Gabe's other hand. LET'S GO!
INT. GREEN ROOM - DAY
Everyone shuffles inside, amped-up and bewildered.
Turn the PA down! He closes the door, Sam's phone still RINGING. The Drummer reaches inside a counter cabinet and slides the fader on a two-channel MIXING BOARD wired to a '90s STEREO.
They didn't lock it. Tiger sees the body, the knife, gestures to Reece and Sam.
You were right there!
Until I wasn'tGABE (hand up) Quiet!
